title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare
summary: Cloudflare's Eval++ is a compute primitive that enables sandboxed execution of LLM-generated code with explicit capability grants. Built on Durable Objects and Dynamic Workers, it provides addressable, persistent, stateful compute with low latency suitable for AI agents.
writeup: Sunil Pai and Matt Carrie present Cloudflare's Eval++, a compute primitive that runs LLM-generated code inside sandboxed isolates using Dynamic Workers, with explicit capability grants governing what that code can access. The design builds on Durable Objects to deliver addressable, persistent, stateful compute at low latency. For AI agents, the system adds resumable streaming and multi-tab sync, addressing how generated code executes safely and how agent state persists across sessions.
